text:
There once was a little ant. One sunny day, this little ant decided it wanted to climb to the top of the giant oak tree nearby.

The tree was massive; its branches stretched far above the shrubbery. The ant took tiny steps and crawled carefully along the bark. It paused to admire the autumn leaves. It snuck through tiny cracks in the bark. Then it crawled a tiny bit further. With effort and perseverance, it reached the very tip of the highest branch.

Now the ant felt triumphant. It had reached its goal. The tree was empty above. The ant felt like it could fly. It took a swift leap. Twisted and pulled... nothing happened! The ant landed with a tiny splat right among the autumn leaves.

"MISTAKE!" It exclaimed to itself.

The determined little ant kept trying. After all, it had come this far. First, it tried to swing. No luck. Then it tried scattering leaves. Still didn't work. It picked
